frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Learning theory framing animal choice via reward-prediction updates.

frameworks.name: action-value learning model
frameworks.one_line: Modified Q-learning variants capture win-stay-lose-switch and perseveration.

frameworks.name: multistep Markov model
frameworks.one_line: Baseline sequence predictor benchmarked against value-learning models.

core_question: Which learning model best predicts rat free-choice sequences, and how are action values represented across nucleus accumbens and ventral pallidum?
key_assumptions: Choice behavior is driven by latent action values updated by reward
Action values can be estimated from behavior and read out from neural activity
Corticobasal ganglia circuit encodes decision variables
manipulations: Free-choice task with rats across trials
Comparison across multiple behavioral learning algorithms
Neural recordings in nucleus accumbens vs ventral pallidum
key_findings: Modified action-value learning models outperformed best multistep Markov baseline
Models captured win-stay-lose-switch and perseverating choice strategies
Action-value and future-action information coded in both NAc and VP
Value coding was less dominant than coding of trial type, selected action, and reward outcome
interpretations: Action-value learning provides a better account of rat choice than sequence statistics
Ventral striatopallidal circuit carries decision variables but is dominated by action/outcome signals
